titleStmt.title: Stone pillar from Myazedi pagoda, Myinkaba, Pagan (A)

licence.p: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 Unported Licence. To view a copy of the licence, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_idListMembers_v01.xml file.

div.ab:
śrī . namo buddhāya . pu rhā skhaṅ· sāsanā °anhac· ta
c· thoṅ· khrok· ryā nhac· chāy· het· nhac· lon·
liy· brī ra kā . °īy· °arimaddanapur· mañ· su prañ·
nhik· °ā . śrītribuvanādityadhammarāj· mañ· su ma
ṅ· phlac· °e°· . thiv· maṅ· °e°· pāy· ma yā ta
c· yok· su kā trilokavaṭaṁsakādevī
mañ· °e°· . thiv· pāy· ma yā sā tamu leh· rāja
kumār· mañ· °e°· . thiv· maṅ· kā kyon· suṁ rvoh·
teh· pāy· ma yā °ā piy· °e°· . thiv· pāy· ma
yā syī· kha ra kā . thiv· pāy· ma yā tan· chā nhaṅ'· thi
v· kyon· suṁ rvoh· su nhaṅ'· teh· thiv· pāy· ma yā
sā °a sā rājakumār· mañ· so °ā maṅ· piy· tuṁ
°e°· . thuiv· maṅ· °anhac· nhac· chāy het nhac· maṅ· mū bri ru
y'a °e°· . siy· kha mū nā su rhov· nhik· teh· . thuiv· rājaku
mār· mañ· su pay· ma yā sā mi mī keiv· muy· so maṅ·
grī klañ jo °ok· mi ra kā . rhuy· °ati su pu rhā skhaṅ· °a
chaṅ· plu ruy'· °e°· nhap· liy· su rhov· teh °iy· si
°· min'· °e°· . °iy· rhuy· pu rhā kā ṅa skhaṅ· °aphei°· °ati°· ky
on· plo°· su teh· . kyon· suṁ rvoh· °atui°· kyo
n· ṅa skhaṅ· piy· su sañ· kā °iy· rhuy· pu rhā°ā °atui°· kyo
n· piy· ye°· . thiv· rhov· teh· maṅ· nhac· klui°· ra kā koṅ·
lheṅ'· teh· koṅ· lheṅ'· teh· min'· ruy'· °e°· saṅ grī
mahāther· . saṅ grī muggaliputtatissatther· . saṅ grī su
medha paṇḍit· . saṅ grī brahmapāl· . saṅ grī brahmadi
v· . saṅ grī son· . saṅ grī saṅghasena varapaṇḍi
t· . thuiv· skhaṅ· tui°· °amhok· teh· maṅ· riy· son· °e°·
. thiv· brī ra kā thuiv· rājakumār· mañ· su pay· ma yā °a sā
thiv· rhuy· pu rhā thāpanā ruy'· °e°· °iy· rhuy· °athot· mū so
kū plo°· °e°· . plo°· brī ra kā °iy· kū pu rhā lhot·
su rhov· nhik· teh· . sak· mu na lon· tac· rvoh· . ra pā
y· tac· rvoh· . hen· buiv· tac· rvoh· . °iy· kyon·
suṁ rvoh· yo ruy'· °e°· . thuiv· rājakumār· mañ· su pay·
ma yā°· sā °iya kū pu rhā °ā riy· son· ruy'· °e°· °iy°· sei
°· min'· °e°· . °iy· ṅā °amho°· kā sarvvañutañā
ṇ· prajñā ra °am'· sū °akroṅ· phlac· ciy'· teh· . ṅa
noṅ· °ā ṅa sā lañ· goṅ· . ṅa mliy· lañ· goṅ· . ṅa °achu
y· lañ· goṅ· . sū tac· thū lañ· goṅ· . °iy· pu rhā
°ā ṅā lhū kha su kyon· °anhip· °acak· teh· mū mu
kā °arimittiryā pu rhā skhaṅ· °aphū ra ciy· . = .

. śrī . buddhādikaṁ vatthuvaraṁ namitvā puññaṁ kataṁ yaṁ jinasā
sanasmiṁ°anādikaṁ rājakumāranāmadheyyena vakkhā
mi sunātha me taṁ . nibbānā lokanāthassa aṭṭhavī
sādhike gate sahasse pana vassānaṁ chasate cāpare ta
thā .°arimaddananāmasmi pure °āsi mahabbalo rājā
tibhuvanādicco °udiccādiccavaṁsajo . tassāse
kā piyā devi sā tilokavataṁsakā hi
tesī kusalā sabbakiccesu pana rājino . ta
ssāseko suto rājakumāro nāma nāma
to °amacco rājakiccesu byāvato satimā
vidū .°adā gāmattayaṁ tassā deviyā so ma
hipati pasanno sabbadā dāsaparibhogena bhuñjituṁ
°aniccatāvasaṁ tassā gatāya pana deviyā rā
ja rājakumārassa °adā gāmattaya puna . aṭṭhavīsa
ti vassāni rajjaṁ dhammena kāriya māranantikarogassa
vasaṁ patte narādhipe . saranto dhammarājassa mahantaṁ gu
ṇasañcayaṁ kāretvā satthuno bimbaṁ sabbasovaṇṇa
yaṁ subhaṁ . gahetvā taṁ mahantena sakkārena sumānaso
°upasaṅkamma rājānaṁ °āha cintitam °attano . bhāgaṁ katvā
nidaṁ satthubimbaṁ sovaṇṇayaṁ subhaṁ °akāsiṁ vo va
raṁ puññaṁ sāmi tumhe numodatha . gāmattayaṁ pi vo
sāmi pubbe dinnan tu me °ahaṁ °imasseva munindassa demi ta
ñ cānumodatha .°evaṁ vutte mahīpālo roge
nāturamānaso sādhu sādhu ti vatvāna tuṭṭhahattho
pamodito . dayāparo mahāthero thero muggali
puttako sumedhatta sumedho ti laddhanāmo ca paṇḍito
brahmapālo tathā brahmadevo sampannasīlavā sono
bahussuto saṁghasenavho varapaṇḍito .°etesaṁ pa
na bhikkhūnaṁ saṁmukhā so sumānaso jalaṁ pātesi katvāna sa
kkhin tu vasudhātalaṁ . tato so taṁ mahāmacco bimbaṁ sova
ṇṇayaṁ subhaṁ patiṭṭhāpiya kāresi guhaṁ kañcanathūpikaṁ .
katvāna maṅgalaṁ buddhapatimāya guhāya ca °akāsevaṁ paṇī
dhānaṁ nibbinno bhavasaṅkate . karontena mayā °etaṁ yaṁ pu
ññaṁ taṁ samācitaṁ hotu sabbaññutañāṇapativedhā
ya paccayo . yattakā tu mayā dāsā gāmattayanivā
sino dinnā guhāya sovaṇṇapatimāya mahesi
no . putto me vā paputto vā °añño vā pana ñā
tako yo kocipāpasaṁkappo naro °assaddha
mānaso . kareyyupadduvaṁ tesaṁ dāsānaṁ so narādhamo
metteyyadipadindassa dassanaṁ nāthigacchatū
ti .○.

. śrī . namo buddhāya . śrī . sās kyek· buddha tirley
kuli °ār· moy· lṅim· turov klam· ḅār· cvas diñcām· cnām
tuy· . ḍey· ḍuṅ· °arimaddanapur· vo'· smiṅ· śrī tribhuvanādi
tyadhammarāj das· . gna kyek· smiṅ· goḥh· moy· tri
lokavaṭaṁsakādevī °imo'· . kon· gna kyek· goḥ
h· rājakumār· °imo'· . smiṅ· goḥh· kil· ḍik· pi tvā
ñ· ku gna kyek· goḥh· . kāl· gna kyek· goḥh· cuti
°ār· . °a'ut· kiryā gna kyek· goḥ ku ḍik· pi tvāñ· goḥ
smiṅ· tun· kil· ku kon· gna kyek· ma °imo'· rājaku
mār goḥ . smiṅ· goḥh· kmin· ḅār· cvas· diñcām· cnām· tuy· goḥ
kāl· smiṅ· goḥ 'jey· ñan· scuti . kon· gna kyek· ma °i
mo'· rājakumār· goḥ mirnās· guṇ· ma smiṅ· °iñcim·
jirku kindaṁ kyek· thar· moy· °ār· tuḅok· smiṅ· mu
nas· rov· vo'· . kyek· thar· vo'· °ey· dik· pa raṁ
po' tirla ḍik pi tvāñ· ma tirla kil· ku °ey· goḥh·
ey· dik· kil· ku kyek· vo'· tirla °anumodanā da'·
. kāl· goḥ smiṅ· sḍik· gap· pumas· thic· °ā thic· °ā smiṅ· pa
sādhukār· . kāl· goḥh· tirla poy mhāther· . ticā
r· muggaliputtatissatther· . ticār· sumedhapaṇḍit· . ti
cār· brahmapāl· . ticār· brahmadiv· . ticār· son·
. ticār· saṅghasenavarapaṇḍit· . kinta tirla
ta goḥ smiṅ· cut· ḍek· han· ti . blaḥ goḥ kon· gna kye
k· ma °imo'· rājakumār· goḥ ket· kyek· thar· goḥ
thāpanā kandaṁ guoh· cloṅ· thar· vo'· . kāl· busac· kye
k guoh· vo'· kon· gna kyek· goḥ ket· sak·muna
lor· moy· tvāñ· . rapāy moy· tvāñ· . ñaḥh· gir'u
y moy· tvāñ· . °a'ut· ḍik· pi tvāñ· goḥ cut· ḍek· ku
kyek· thar· ma māpanā hin· goḥ vo'· rādhanā rov· vo'·
. sinraṅ· °e'· vo'· or· das· het· ku gvo'· sarvvañuta
ñaṇ· . kon· °ey· laḥ . cov· °ey· laḥ . kulo
ey· laḥ . ñaḥ c'eṅ· laḥ . yal· pa X °upadrov· ku ḍi
k· ma °ey· kil· ku kyek· vo'· yaṅ· ñir·ñāc· kye
k· trey· mettey· laḥ or· ḍeh· go'· ||0||

1 . siri . dathagaṃda ḅa doṃ ḅaṁḥ ḅiṁḥ pduṃ sgu daṃḥ ḅa tva 1000 600
20 hraṁ °o sniḥ ḅiṁḥ tvaṃṁḥ tha daṃṁ // yaṁ tiṁ priḥ rimadhanarbu °o rmi ḅiṁḥ si //
sri tribhuvaṃnadiṃṁtya dhamaraja °o rmi ḅiṁḥ si // °o doṃḥ ḍaZ ḅaṁḥ °o rvaṃḥ ma
yaḥ triḍogavaṃdasaga deṃviṃ ḅiṁḥ si °o rmi // pau °o saḥ rajaguma ḅiṁḥ
si °o rmi // °o vaṁ traḥ kra nhoḥ ḅiṁḥ paṁḥ toḥZ // po ḅaṁḥ ma yaḥ ḅiṁḥ hi ta daṃṁ
mayaḥ °o dra traḥ kra hoḥ ḅiṁḥ paṁḥ tḅaḥ ḅaṁḥ ma yaḥ °o saḥ rajaguma °o vaṁ //
pau ḅaṁḥ tdaṃḥ sniḥ nsu hraṁ biṁḥ ta daṃṁ // ḅiṁḥ sriḥ ḅiṁḥ hniṁḥ hḍiṁḥ hi °o mtu duṃ
roḥ // po ḅaṁḥ ma yaḥ °o saḥ rajaguma ḅiṁḥ si °o rmi // °o diṃṁ
ḅiṁḥ mtau ma pau tdaṃḥ to °o kḍeḥ troḥ diṃṁ ḅiṁḥ mdauṃ haḥ ḍaḥ daṃṁ // pau ḅaṁḥ
ḅudha °o chaḥ bo bradima tha tlu ḅiṁḥ se kyaḥ // pau ḅaṁḥ ḅudha biṁḥ tuḥ
thmuḥ ḍoḥ yaṁ na ḅiṁḥ tdiṃḥ toḥZ // yaṁ ḅaṁḥ ḅudha tha ḅaṁḥ raḥ saḥ biṁḥ
se ma ḅuḥ ḅaṁḥ °o vaṁ paṁḥ ce choḥZ // yaṁ traḥ kra hoḥ ḅiṁḥ paṁḥ
ma ḅuḥ // yaṁ baṁḥ hra tha °o vaṁ paṁḥ cheZ // pau ḍoḥ baṁḥ tdaṃḥ ḅiṁḥ kiṁ
pha daṃṁ ḅiṁḥ ṅa ha pra choḥ ha pra choḥ ḅiṁḥ si // pau °o doṃḥ traḥ ḅaṁḥ
mahaṭhe /traḥ ḅaṁḥ mugaṃḍubudadisaṭhe /traḥ ḅaṁḥ ⟪su⟫medhabadiṃṁ
/ traḥ ḅaṁḥ vrahmaba /traḥ ḅaṁḥ vradaṃyoḥ /traḥ ḅaṁḥ su /traḥ ḅaṁḥ
sagaṃsivaṃrabadiṃṁ // pau traḥ ḅaṁḥ sagha tvo °o hṅa diṁ duṃ tdaṃḥ
tu ḅaṁḥ ḅiṁḥ cha toḥ tduṃ // pau ḅiṁḥ ta daṃṁ ma yaḥ °o saḥ raja
guma ḅiṁḥ si °o rmi ma // ḅiṁḥ stabana budha tha ḅiṁḥ se goṃ °o stau tha ḅiṁḥ
ta daṃṁ // pau goṃ °o hḍo ḅiṁḥ saṁḥ roḥ // samanarḍoḥ kra taṁ /ra bai kra taṁ / jiṁ
vuḥ kra taṁ // yaṁ traḥ kra hoḥ diṃṁ ḅiṁḥ diṃṁ daṃṁ // yaṁ ḅaṁḥ ma yaḥ
rajaguma yaṁ goṃ ḅudha °o vaṁ tduṃ ḅiṁḥ chai ta daṃṁ // yaṁ na ḅiṁḥ diṃṁ cho
ma gaṁḥ pra ḅuḥ saveñudeña breña ḅiṁḥ ḅiṁḥ paṁḥ che naḥ tiṁ pḍaṁḥ paZ //
yaṁ tra tiṁ mtu knaṁḥ duṃ gi saḥ ḍa /gi pli la gi sruḥ ḍaZ mra ja hṅa ḍa yaṁ
dha °o vaṁ gaṁḥ hḍiṁḥ toḥ ma diṃṁ /ga hñiṁ chi ga bro pdaṃ ma taḥ ṅuḥ ḅuḥ //
yaṁ baṁḥ ḅudha °arimedeyaṃ daṃṁ ḅaḥ diṃ chiṁḥ tiṁ tmu ma paṁḥ che choḥ //Z

app.note: One could perhaps alternatively read rmi. Cf. PYU 16, l. 2d, and our note. But the vowel diacritic used here and throughout in 7-8 corresponds to one of the forms of -ī seen in Nagarjunakonda inscription.
Krech: la (with horizontal stroke above); Blagden also reads ḍa but in note recognizes the possibility of ḷa, ignoring the superscript line.
Blagden: voṃḥ, but unsure of the vowel; Krech: vaṃṁḥ, after due consideration of Blagden's voṃḥ. There is possibly a subscript consonant below the syllable here in A.

div.p: Quadrilingual text. Records building of a shrine by Prince Rājakumāra, son of Kyanzittha kyan cac sāḥ, and dedication of three villages and slaves thereto.
Blagden reads our °o as °u; Krech reads it as ʔa.
For phonological symmetry (and to eliminate the long vowel ū), Krech reads Blagden's long ū as av (and -va as av?). We read short u for ū in these words.
Problem of Blagden's ḷ, which we provisionally read as ḍ.
A = A pillar (7); B = B pillar (8).
Z = superscript slanted stroke not found in any other Pyu texts.
Two types of punctuation sign: plain daṇḍa (/) and ra-shaped daṇḍa (|).
1. sgu: sguṃ B; Krech chose sguṃ for his composite text.

1. Blagden, following Cœdès' suggestion (review in BEFEO 1911, pp. 435–6), identified the last three akṣaras of line 1 and the first of line 2 as Indian symbols for 1000, 600 and 20 respectively. Cœdès included hraṁ as a symbol for 8, but Blagden, citing the presence of anusvāra and likely TB cognates, argued otherwise. On the old system of additive/multiplicative number signs used here, see Salomon 1998: 56–60. Luce (PPPB I, 140–2), in reference to his plates 35 and 36 showing such signs engraved on Pyu bricks, discusses, at some length, the non place-value number signs found in Pyu inscribed materials, and starts with those at the end of lines 1 of 007/8. He correctly represents the sign for 600 as consisting of a sign for 100 with a small appendix dangling from the lower right of the basic 100 sign: this appendix is currently lost by damage in 007, but faintly visible on RTI in the case of 8. Although he correctly reads 600, Cœdès rather gravely misrepresents how this sign is composed: ‘On ne peut pas tirer grand chose du groupe très ruiné qui termine la première ligne et qui correspond à 600: M. Blagden propose de lire jha, hña ou jhña ; j'y verrais plutôt st plus une voyelle, groupe servant justement à représenter les centaines à partie de 300 (Kielhorn, Report [on a the Search for Sanskrit MSS. in the Bombay Presidency], 1880/1881, p. VIII et suiv.).' Clear instances of 600 as composed of recognizable signs for 100 and 6 can be found in the Kusuma inscription from Rajasthan in India (Epigraphia Indica 36, 1965–66: 47–9) of a year 693, with date is expressed as 600 90 3 in the last line. (If the era intended here is the so-called Vikrama era, as has been assumed, the inscription dates to ca. 635/6 CE.). Further instances were pointed out to us by Seishi Karashima in the folio numbering of Sanskrit manuscript found at Dunhuang but probably copied in the Kashmir area, now held at the British Library (IOL San 1492/59 and 1492/61, transliteration pubished by S. Karashima et al. in British Library Sanskrit Fragments III.2, pp. 786f. and 793f., 2015). Here we see the numbers 613 and 676 (expressed as 600 10 3 and 600 70 6) in the margins. While the inscription from Rajasthan shows 600 clearly decomposable into linear elements 100 and 6, the manuscript specimens show the 100 and 6 elements merged vertically. As apparently understood by Luce, this is is also what has happened with our 600 signs in 007/8: the shape of element 6 in this case is roughly the same as that shown in Luce's chart Z in PPPB ItI.

2. mī: mi[ṅ·] B. One could perhaps alternatively read rmi. Cf. PYU 16, l. 2d, and our note. But the vowel diacritic used here and throughout in 7-8 corresponds to one of the forms of -ī seen in Nagarjunakonda inscription.

. Tbe quadrilingual inscription was first mentioned by 9, 83, 108; that work includes an estampage of the Pali and Pyu faces (“texte en langue inconnue”). (A) is a detailed study of the Pyu face, with estampages, a complete transliteration and translation, notes on where the A pillar and the B pillar diverge, a glossary and analysis of script and language. I, 1, 59-68 (A) makes some changes to his transliteration and provides a better glossary. Blagden’s work provides the basis for 320-37,340-44, 357-63 linguistic study of the text (that leans heavily on comparison with the Pali face) and which culminates in a new transliteration, word-for-word glosses and a full translation. 53-61 includes his own transliteration (in Burmese script) and an English translation. Luce, in I, 62-4, 74 n. 15, provides a brief overview. 153-65 (A) is a complete re-reading of the Pyu face, with epigraphical and linguistic analysis, but with some odd readings and phonological re-interpretations. Re-edited here from published photographs and our team’s RTIs.
